verdict_rationale: B1 and B2 from the prior review are genuinely closed by the fix-up commits. However NS1 introduces a new blocking defect: the Task 1 Step 3 explicit git add enumeration omits ~10 files that the prose mandates be updated in the same commit (No-Legacy policy on ValidationCheck/ValidationError construction sites). A literal executor follows the git add list and ships a broken build with TypeError at ~20 call sites across service.py, composer/service.py, and six test files.

B1_loadSnapshot_race.verification: All three required scenarios are handled by the auditReadinessStore.ts implementation in commit aa037c6f8. Scenario 1 (no cancellation, slow v=1 resolves after fast v=2): the set() closure re-reads state.snapshotsBySession[sessionId] at resolution time and checks current.composition_version > snapshot.composition_version, discarding the stale v=1. Scenario 2 (AbortController cancels v=1 when v=2 starts): prev.abort() fires before the new controller is installed; the AbortError is caught by the name==='AbortError' guard and returns without writing. Scenario 3 (write guard rejects stale even if the abort did not fire): same read-current-state mechanism as Scenario 1. The monotonic-guard test in auditReadinessStore.test.ts ('loadSnapshot monotonic guard: fast-v2 + slow-v1 interleaved') covers Scenario 1 explicitly. The AbortController paths are exercised implicitly through clearSession and the version-advance branch. No gap found.
B1_loadSnapshot_race.residual_note: fetchAuditReadinessExplain (the explain endpoint) does NOT receive an AbortController signal. The store's loadExplain function has no abort path. This is an asymmetry: snapshot fetches are abortable, explain fetches are not. If a user opens the ExplainDialog and immediately navigates away, the explain fetch resolves and writes to explainsBySession for the old sessionId. Impact is cosmetic (stale cached narrative shown on re-entry until version advances), not correctness-critical. Downgraded to a warning (see NW1).

B2_handleValidate_deletion_ahead_of_Phas.verification: Phase 2C Task 8 was scope-shrunk: only the standalone Validate button UI is removed. handleValidate, injectSystemMessage, sendValidationFeedback, canValidate, and isValidating remain wired in InspectorPanel.tsx. No regression window exists even if Phase 3A never lands — the handlers continue to function. The Task 8.5 describe.skip guard test documents the Phase 3A handoff contract in code and provides a mechanical unskip ceremony for the Phase 3A PR author. This is documentation-level enforcement rather than the 'fails red until relocation lands' gate the prior review recommended, but because the underlying regression is structurally eliminated (handlers are retained), the skip guard is sufficient.
B2_handleValidate_deletion_ahead_of_Phas.residual_note: See NS3: the skip guard is softer than ideal. But with handlers retained, the hard failure mode (silent loss of LLM validation feedback) cannot occur. Downgraded from BLOCKER to WARNING.

W1_EXTERNAL_CALL_drift_unidirectional.note: The test_every_external_call_plugin_is_on_allowlist_or_explicitly_excepted test is present and correctly catches forward drift (new EXTERNAL_CALL plugin added without allowlist update). The inverse (plugin makes external calls without marker) remains undetected. Acknowledged in trust.py module docstring in the revised plan. Acceptable for Phase 2 — no new mechanism added, no regression from prior state.

W2_no_AbortController.note: AbortController added to fetchAuditReadiness and loadSnapshot. fetchAuditReadinessExplain still has no abort path — see NW1.

W3_demo_readiness_button_removal.note: The plan notes the Validate button removal as a demo risk but does not defer Phase 2C. This is a product decision, not a plan defect. Still a demo-prep concern but no systems issue.

W4_three_api_modules_duplicating_helpers.note: auditReadiness.ts JSDoc explicitly names the technical debt and defers to Phase 8. No change from prior state.

new_blocking_issues.title: Task 1 Step 3 git add enumeration omits ~10 required co-update files — No-Legacy violation
new_blocking_issues.description: 14a Task 1 Step 2b prose correctly states 'CLAUDE.md No-Legacy requirement: every call site is updated in this single commit. Do not defer any call site to a later task.' The grep audit (grep -rn 'ValidationCheck(' src/ tests/ and grep -rn 'ValidationError(' src/ tests/) is prescribed. However, the explicit git add block in Step 3 only enumerates 7 files: audit_readiness/__init__.py, models.py, config.py, execution/schemas.py, execution/validation.py, and two new test files. It does NOT enumerate the files containing the ~10+ existing call sites that must also be updated. A literal executor will run the git add as written and commit a broken state where the new required fields (affected_nodes on ValidationCheck, error_code on ValidationError) are declared with no defaults but all existing call sites remain missing the new args — causing TypeError at every legacy construction site and ~20+ test failures.

production_files_omitted: src/elspeth/web/execution/service.py (1 ValidationCheck + 1 ValidationError site at line 657/664)
src/elspeth/web/composer/service.py (1 ValidationCheck + 1 ValidationError site at lines 1071/1073)
blast_radius.count_note: validation.py itself is listed in the git add (already has ~27 ValidationCheck sites and ~15 ValidationError sites that need affected_nodes=() and error_code=None added respectively). The audit grep will surface the non-validation.py sites, but the git add as written excludes them from the commit.

new_blocking_issues.failure_mode: Build breaks immediately post-commit. All test runs in CI and in the TDD red->green cycles will show TypeError at legacy call sites. Not silent — loud fail. But a literal-executor agent following the plan step-by-step will produce this broken intermediate state and may not understand why the test suite fails since it isn't asserting the new fields yet at Step 3.
new_blocking_issues.recommended_fix: Expand the Step 3 git add block to explicitly enumerate all files containing existing ValidationCheck and ValidationError construction sites. Alternatively, change the instruction to 'git add $(git diff --name-only)' after the grep-and-update pass, which is self-documenting. Also consider: the mypy check in Step 3 covers src/elspeth/web/audit_readiness/ and src/elspeth/web/execution/schemas.py but not src/elspeth/web/execution/service.py, src/elspeth/web/composer/service.py, or the test files. Extend the mypy check to cover all modified files.

new_warnings.title: loadExplain has no AbortController — ExplainDialog fetches are non-cancellable
new_warnings.description: The fix-up added AbortController to loadSnapshot (snapshot fetches). loadExplain in auditReadinessStore.ts has no abort path: no signal is passed to fetchAuditReadinessExplain, and clearSession only aborts the AbortController stored in abortControllers[sessionId] which is only set by loadSnapshot. If a user opens the ExplainDialog while a session is active, then navigates away (triggering clearSession), the in-flight explain fetch resolves and writes to explainsBySession[oldSessionId]. On return to the old session, the cached narrative is stale but will be rendered without refetch because cached.composition_version === compositionVersion short-circuits the reload. Impact: minor correctness exposure (stale narrative rendered on re-entry). Not an audit-correctness failure — the narrative is explanatory prose, not a decision record. Demo risk: low (requires specific navigation pattern to reproduce).

new_warnings.title: Task 8.5 guard is describe.skip, not a failing-red CI gate
new_warnings.description: The prior review (B2, C3) recommended a test that 'FAILS if Phase 3A Task 8 has not landed.' The implemented Task 8.5 guard is describe.skip — it registers as 1 skipped in CI and never fails red. The unskip ceremony is documented in a TODO comment that Phase 3A's PR author must read and act on. This is a documentation-level contract, not a mechanical enforcement. If Phase 3A's executor does not read the comment, the skip persists indefinitely and the contract is silently violated. However, because B2's underlying hazard is structurally closed (handlers retained in InspectorPanel.tsx, no regression window), the skip guard is an acceptable documentation device rather than a hard gate. The remaining risk is that Phase 3A's PR forgets to unskip — producing a permanently-skipped test that rotates into noise.

new_warnings.title: NS7 — 'Audit ready' collapse with multiple not_applicable rows may mislead
new_warnings.description: The panel collapses to 'Audit ready ✓' when all rows are ok or not_applicable. In Phase 2, the Retention, LLM interpretations, and (when no secrets) Secrets rows are all not_applicable. A user (or demo audience) reading 'Audit ready ✓' on a minimal pipeline may interpret this as 'this pipeline is fully audit-ready' when in fact three rows have deferred evaluation. The design spec text 'not_applicable' is semantically distinct from 'ok' but the collapsed summary erases that distinction. For the demo this is a risk: a sophisticated evaluator may probe 'what does Audit ready mean if retention is not configured?' and the answer visible in the collapsed state is insufficient.
new_warnings.recommendation: Consider adding a secondary label in the collapsed state when any row is not_applicable, e.g. 'Audit ready — 3 checks not configured'. This does not require a backend change; the collapsed-state logic in AuditReadinessPanel.tsx already reads all rows. Alternatively, document the not_applicable semantics explicitly in the panel's accessible label so screen-reader users and technically literate audience members understand it does not mean 'audited and confirmed'.

new_warnings.title: NS5 — dangling canValidate/isValidating with eslint-disable creates lint debt that compounds across phases
new_warnings.description: Task 8 Step 5 instructs: if canValidate or isValidating become unused after button removal, suppress lint warnings with eslint-disable-next-line + a TODO comment. This is intentional and documented. However, each eslint-disable is a suppression that persists until Phase 3A's cleanup. If Phase 3A drifts or is rescoped, the suppressions become permanent noise. Additionally, future eslint runs against InspectorPanel.tsx will report 0 warnings (because they are suppressed) — masking the presence of dead code from routine lint review. The CLAUDE.md pattern is to delete rather than suppress; this is an acknowledged exception but the exception has a phase-scoped expiry that depends on Phase 3A landing.

new_warnings.title: NS8 — backend-only rollout of ValidationError schema change is safe but plan does not document it
new_warnings.description: Adding error_code: str | None to ValidationError is additive: existing JSON payloads that reach frontend TypeScript code will have the new field present (from backend) or absent (from pre-Phase-2A stored JSON). The TypeScript interface in 14b does not yet mirror error_code (it is a backend-only field surfaced only through the audit-readiness API, not returned directly to the frontend in the validation endpoint). However, validation results stored in the audit trail (guided/audit.py:249 stores dict(validation_result)) and in the Landscape's `runtime_preflight` field will have the new fields present after Phase 2A deploys. Pre-Phase-2A persisted records will lack the fields. Per the DB migration policy (operator deletes old DB on deploy) this is not a deserialization problem. The plan does not document this explicitly — an executor who reads the plan without the memory context would not know whether a DB wipe is required.
affected_files: src/elspeth/web/composer/guided/audit.py line 249 (persists dict(validation_result) to audit trail)

new_checks_assessment.NS1_ValidationError_affected_nodes_blast: BLOCKING — see NB1. git add enumeration gap.
new_checks_assessment.NS2_scoped_secret_resolver_Protocol_chan: CLOSED. ReadinessService is a new class with no prior callers or test fixtures. The Protocol was corrected to match scoped_secret_resolver (list_refs(user_id) only). No test update blast radius exists.
new_checks_assessment.NS3_Task85_mechanical_guard: WARNING — see NW2. Skip guard is documentation-level; underlying hazard closed by scope-shrink.
new_checks_assessment.NS4_plan_complexity: NOT BLOCKING. 290 + 99 + 163 = 552 lines of additions across sub-plans is large but each sub-plan is independently executable by a single agent. The TDD shape (red → green → commit per task) bounds cognitive load per unit of work. The concern is real but manageable given the existing agent infrastructure.
new_checks_assessment.NS6_backpressure_under_rapid_edits: NOT BLOCKING. AbortController abort-previous provides implicit backpressure: each new composition version aborts the prior in-flight fetch, bounding concurrent backend snapshot fetches to at most 1 in-flight + 1 pending per session. A power user typing fast will issue sequential fetches with abort-cancel on each advance; backend load is bounded by RTT, not by typing speed. No debounce needed unless profiling shows saturation.
new_checks_assessment.NS8_deploy_coordination: WARNING — see NW5. Backend-only rollout is safe; plan does not document it.

feedback_loop_delta.FL3_compute_snapshot_latency: UNCHANGED — still a delayed-feedback loop mitigated by version-keyed cache.

dependency_chain_delta.Phase2C_to_Phase3A: Risk reduced. Handlers retained in InspectorPanel.tsx; Phase 3A deferral does not create a regression window. Phase 3A's scope is still required for cleanup but is no longer load-bearing for correctness.
dependency_chain_delta.Phase2_to_Phase3B_panel_relocation: UNCHANGED from prior W5. Prose-documented constraint; gated by 15b2 Task 9 Step 4a test. Still a warning.
dependency_chain_delta.Phase2_to_Phase5b_llm_interpretations: UNCHANGED. One-line flip, deferral marker in service.py. Low risk.
dependency_chain_delta.NB1_git_add_gap: New dependency chain issue: the co-update commit that adds affected_nodes and error_code must include all ~10 omitted files or CI breaks on the commit that introduces the new required fields.
